title: acer monspessulanum var. illyricum
intro: Acer monspessulanum var. illyricum is a lesser-known variety of the European maple tree, scientifically classified as a type of maple tree, native to parts of Europe. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Acer monspessulanum var. illyricum include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
